message:
Fix Codex CLI install by adding pinned version fallback

npm can return 404 briefly after a new version is published.
Both install.sh and update.sh now use a 3-tier fallback chain:
1. @openai/codex@latest (preferred)
2. @openai/codex (unversioned)
3. @openai/codex@0.87.0 (pinned, reliable)

Fixes bd-3hg8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-zwg3","title":"TASK: Add final completion certificate to onboard","description":"# TASK: Add final completion certificate to onboard

## Context
When user completes all 9 lessons, show a special completion screen.

## Proposed Screen
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                             │
│  🏆 ACFS ONBOARDING COMPLETE!                               │
│                                                             │
│  Congratulations! You've completed all 9 lessons.           │
│                                                             │
│  You're now ready to:                                       │
│  • Launch AI agents with cc, cod, gmi                       │
│  • Manage sessions with ntm                                 │
│  • Search code with rg                                      │
│  • Use the full flywheel workflow                           │
│                                                             │
│  Next steps:                                                │
│  • Run 'acfs info' for quick reference                      │
│  • Run 'acfs cheatsheet' for all commands                   │
│  • Start a project with 'ntm new myproject && cc'           │
│                                                             │
│  Completed: $(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')                       │
│                                                             │
│  Happy coding! 🚀                                            │
│                                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation

### Store Completion Timestamp
```bash
# In onboard_progress.json
{
  "completed_lessons": [0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8],
  "completed_at": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z"
}
```

### Trigger Condition
Show when:
- User just completed lesson 8 (last)
- OR user runs `onboard` when all lessons complete

### Re-access
User can re-run `onboard` to see certificate again.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Certificate shown on final completion
- [ ] Completion timestamp stored
- [ ] Shows next steps recommendations
- [ ] Accessible by running onboard again
- [ ] Celebration emoji/styling","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-22T19:59:54.304099Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T20:35:03.037145Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T20:35:03.037145Z","close_reason":"View Certificate menu option added to onboard.  {"id":"bd-3vx8","title":"Optimize runtime install phases (apt/bun/cargo/etc) based on profiling","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:39.996743538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T19:25:27.980431613Z","close_reason":"Implemented batched cargo install optimization in install_languages_legacy_tools. Previously, 5 cargo tools (du-dust, lsd, bat, fd-find, ripgrep) were installed sequentially, each requiring separate index download and dependency resolution. Now they are collected and batch-installed in a single cargo install command, which: (1) downloads the crate index once instead of 5x, (2) resolves dependencies once, (3) allows cargo to parallelize compilation. Includes fallback to individual installs if batch fails.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vx8","depends_on_id":"bd-2z32","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:00:40.029703844Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -3201,14 +3201,22 @@ install_agents_phase() {
     fi
 
     # Codex CLI (install as target user)
+    # Uses fallback chain: @latest -> unversioned -> pinned 0.87.0
+    # npm can 404 briefly after publishing; pinned version is reliable fallback
     log_detail "Installing Codex CLI for $TARGET_USER"
     try_step "Installing Codex CLI" run_as_target bash -c '
         set -euo pipefail
         bun_bin="$1"
-        if ! "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest; then
-            echo "WARN: Codex CLI latest tag install failed; retrying @openai/codex" >&2
-            "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex
+        CODEX_FALLBACK_VERSION="0.87.0"
+        if "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest 2>/dev/null; then
+            exit 0
+        fi
+        echo "WARN: Codex CLI @latest failed; retrying unversioned" >&2
+        if "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex 2>/dev/null; then
+            exit 0
         fi
+        echo "WARN: Codex CLI unversioned failed; retrying pinned $CODEX_FALLBACK_VERSION" >&2
+        "$bun_bin" install -g --trust "@openai/codex@$CODEX_FALLBACK_VERSION"
     ' _ "$bun_bin" || true
 
     # Create wrapper script that uses bun as runtime (avoids node PATH issues)

@@ -1071,9 +1071,12 @@ update_agents() {
     fi
 
     # Codex CLI via bun (--trust allows postinstall scripts)
+    # Uses fallback chain: @latest -> unversioned -> pinned 0.87.0
+    # npm can 404 briefly after publishing; pinned version is reliable fallback
     if cmd_exists codex || [[ "$FORCE_MODE" == "true" ]]; then
         local codex_bin_local="$HOME/.local/bin/codex"
         local codex_bin_bun="$HOME/.bun/bin/codex"
+        local codex_fallback_version="0.87.0"
 
         capture_version_before "codex"
         run_cmd_bun_with_retry "Codex CLI" "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest
@@ -1083,6 +1086,11 @@ update_agents() {
             run_cmd_bun_with_retry "Codex CLI (fallback)" "$bun_bin" install -g --trust @openai/codex
         fi
 
+        if [[ ! -x "$codex_bin_local" && ! -x "$codex_bin_bun" ]]; then
+            log_item "warn" "Codex CLI" "unversioned failed; retrying pinned $codex_fallback_version"
+            run_cmd_bun_with_retry "Codex CLI (pinned)" "$bun_bin" install -g --trust "@openai/codex@$codex_fallback_version"
+        fi
+
         # Show version change without double-counting
         if capture_version_after "codex"; then
             [[ "$QUIET" != "true" ]] && printf "       ${DIM}%s → %s${NC}\n" "${VERSION_BEFORE[codex]}" "${VERSION_AFTER[codex]}"
